name: Persistence via Startup Folder
notes:
This rule detects when a file with a suspicious extension is created in the startup folder.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Startup Folder Persistence
SIGMA Description: Detects when a file with a suspicious extension is created in the startup folder


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of [vbs or vbe or vba or bat or dll or ps1 or hta or jse or js or lnk]
when the event matches File Directory (custom) contains any of Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup
TD_list: APPLY Persistence via Startup Folder on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
AND when the event matches File Extension (custom) is any of [vbs or vbe or vba or bat or dll or ps1 or hta or jse or js or lnk]
AND when the event matches File Directory (custom) contains any of Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup
RED_list: Event name: Persistence via Startup Folder
Event description: Detects when a file with a suspicious extension is created in the startup folder
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
